post.description: Create a project in the current organization scope and initialize downstream project setup.

hidden.description: Project discoverability. If true, the project is confidential and only visible to project members. If false, the project is discoverable by all organization members. Defaults to false.

membersHiddenFromGuests.description: Member list visibility. If true, the member list is hidden from guests. If false, guests can see the member list. Defaults to false.

post.description: Create a project in the current organization scope and initialize downstream project setup.

hidden.description: Project discoverability. If true, the project is confidential and only visible to project members. If false, the project is discoverable by all organization members. Defaults to false.

membersHiddenFromGuests.description: Member list visibility. If true, the member list is hidden from guests. If false, guests can see the member list. Defaults to false.

post.description:
Import one or more pre-transcribed transcripts into a project in a single call.

Use this endpoint when you already have speakers and turn-level transcripts from an external source (another ASR system, internal tooling, a chat-style interview) and want to load them into CoLoop without re-running transcription.

## Transcript shapes

Each transcript is a flat object with a `type` field:

- `type: "audio"` / `type: "video"` — `media` is required. Each turn must supply `text`; `utterances` may also be supplied when word-level timing is available.
- `type: "text"` — no `media`, no `utterances`. Each turn supplies `text` only. Use for chat-style interviews or screener questions.

## Semantics

- **Partial success** — each transcript in the request is processed independently. The response returns a per-transcript result with status `created`, `skipped`, or `failed`. A failure on one transcript does not abort the batch.
- **Idempotent via `externalId`** — every transcript must carry a caller-provided `externalId` that is unique within the project. Re-submitting the same `externalId` returns `skipped` with the existing `transcriptId` instead of creating a duplicate.
- **Asynchronous media transfer** (audio/video only) — the recording at `media.downloadUrl` is fetched out-of-band after the response is returned. The URL must remain reachable for at least one hour after the request.
- **Asynchronous processing** — once a transcript is created, downstream work (summary, Q&A extraction, concept labeling, indexing) runs in the background. The transcript appears in the project immediately but analysis fields populate over the following minutes.
- **Batch size** — a single request can carry up to 100 transcripts.

## Authorization

The caller must have edit access on the target project.

## Current status

This endpoint currently runs in facade mode: idempotency is honored (re-submitting an `externalId` returns `skipped`), and a small share of submissions return realistic `failed` results so partners can exercise their retry logic. No real transcripts are created. Contact support@coloop.ai before integrating against production traffic.

transcripts.description: Transcripts to import. Each is processed independently. Maximum 100 per request.

externalId.description: Caller-supplied identifier, unique per project, used for idempotency. Re-submitting the same `externalId` returns a `skipped` result with the existing `transcriptId` instead of creating a duplicate.

languageCode.description: BCP-47 language code of the transcript (e.g. `en`, `fr-CA`). Required; `und` (undetermined) is not accepted — specify the transcript language explicitly.

type.description: What kind of transcript this is. `audio` / `video` require `media`; turns may optionally include `utterances` for timing. `text` has no media file; `media` must be omitted and `utterances` on any turn is rejected.

media.description: The audio or video recording associated with this transcript. Required when `type` is `audio` or `video`; must be omitted when `type` is `text`.

downloadUrl.description: URL CoLoop will download the media file from. Must remain accessible for at least one hour after the request.

extension.description: File extension without the leading dot, e.g. `mp4` or `m4a`.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the media file (e.g. `video/mp4`). If omitted, CoLoop resolves it with a `HEAD` request to `downloadUrl`.

downloadHeaders.description: Optional HTTP headers to include when downloading the file (e.g. authorisation tokens for a private bucket).

speakers.description: Speakers appearing in the transcript. Turns reference these by index.

segments.description: Segments the participant belongs to (e.g. demographic group, cohort, customer tier). Created or reused by value within the project.

role.description: The interviewer or moderator, human or AI/bot.

speakerIndex.description: Index into the enclosing transcript's `speakers` array, identifying who is speaking.

text.description: Full text of this speech turn. Always required and treated as canonical, even when `utterances` is provided.

utterances.description: Optional timed sub-units of this speech turn. Omit for turn-level or text-only transcripts. Only valid when the enclosing transcript has `type: audio | video`. When provided, the concatenation of `utterances[].text` must equal `text` exactly, character for character (whitespace included).

text.description: The utterance text as it appears in the enclosing turn's `text`. May be a single word, a phrase, or a full sentence depending on the granularity of the source transcript.

startMs.description: Start time of this utterance, in milliseconds from the start of the media.

endMs.description: End time of this utterance, in milliseconds from the start of the media.

confidence.description: ASR confidence for this utterance, between 0 and 1. Defaults to `1` when omitted.

results.description: One result per transcript in the request, in the same order.

anyOf.description: The transcript was created. Media transfer (if any) and downstream processing run asynchronously.

transcriptId.description: ID of the newly created transcript.

externalId.description: The `externalId` from the request, echoed back for matching.

anyOf.description: A transcript with this `externalId` already exists in the project; no changes were made.

transcriptId.description: ID of the existing transcript that matches `externalId`.

externalId.description: The `externalId` from the request, echoed back for matching.

anyOf.description: This transcript could not be imported. Other transcripts in the batch are unaffected.

externalId.description: The `externalId` from the request, echoed back for matching.

error.description: Structured error. Branch on `errorCode` for specific failure modes and on `retriable` for retry decisions.
